VETERANS HOME OF CALIFORNIA - YOUNTVILLE - SNF

CCN: 555095 · YOUNTVILLE, CA 94599 · Napa County

OverviewRatingsStaffingInspectionsDeficienciesFinancialOwnership & ChainFacility FeaturesRelatedAll Data

Overview

Address
100 CALIFORNIA DRIVE, YOUNTVILLE, CA 94599
Phone
7079444600
Certified beds
274
Avg daily residents
161 (59% of beds filled)
Ownership
Government (state)
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1978-08-01
Setting
Urban
4 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 4/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 3/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 6.69 2.97 8.70 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 1.83 0.52 2.38 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.57 0.66 0.75 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 4.28 1.79 5.57 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 2.40 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.01 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
5.78
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
1.34
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
7.52
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
35.6%
annual
RN turnover
26.0%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.05
near national average
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.76
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-03-07 8 8 0 68 1 68
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-04-11 27 15 13 180 1 180
Total weighted health score
96.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (44)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

41 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
3 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2025-03-07 2025-04-16
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2025-03-07 2025-04-16
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2025-03-07 2025-04-16
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. F 2025-03-07 2025-04-16
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-03-07 2025-04-16
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-03-07 2025-04-16
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-03-07 2025-04-16
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. D 2025-03-07 2025-04-16
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-02-06 2025-02-24
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2024-08-29 2024-09-18
0713 Provide or arrange emergency care by a doctor 24 hours a day. D 2024-08-29 2024-09-18
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-08-29 2024-09-18
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-07-17 2024-07-31
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-04-11 2024-07-03
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2024-04-11 2024-07-03
0637 Assess the resident when there is a significant change in condition D 2024-04-11 2024-07-03
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-04-11 2024-07-03
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-04-11 2024-07-03
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2024-04-11 2024-07-03
0685 Assist a resident in gaining access to vision and hearing services. D 2024-04-11 2024-07-03
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2024-04-11 2024-07-03
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2024-04-11 2024-07-03
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-04-11 2024-07-03
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-04-11 2024-07-03
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-04-11 2024-07-03
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. D 2024-04-11 2024-07-03
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. F 2024-04-11 2024-07-03
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-04-11 2024-07-03
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-03-07 2024-05-15
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-03-07 2024-05-15
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2023-09-25 2023-10-03
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. G 2023-07-05 2023-10-03
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. G 2023-07-05 2023-10-03
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2023-07-05 2023-10-03
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2023-07-05 2023-10-03
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. D 2023-03-16 2023-04-25
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2023-03-16 2023-04-25
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-03-16 2023-04-25
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-03-16 2023-04-25
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2023-03-16 2023-04-25
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2023-03-16 2023-04-25
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2023-03-16 2023-04-25
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2023-03-16 2023-04-25
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. E 2023-03-16 2023-04-25
